items.title: Automate PyPI Publishing for Coding Bridge Agent in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new GitHub Actions workflow that automatically publishes the `coding-bridge-agent` package to PyPI on every push to the main branch. The versioning follows a date-based CalVer format, ensuring unique builds without collisions. This enhancement streamlines the release process and makes the package readily available for users.

items.title: Introducing Coding Bridge Remote Control in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the Coding Bridge feature to Nexior, allowing users to pair a local Coding Bridge agent for remote coding sessions. Key components include a new data layer, a Vuex store for state management, and a user-friendly UI for managing sessions and permissions. The implementation supports full internationalization across 18 locales, enhancing accessibility for a global audience.

items.title: Disable Midjourney by Default in Backend 🚫
items.summary: This update disables the Midjourney feature for newly initialized sites by setting `DEFAULT_FEATURES_MIDJOURNEY.enabled` to `false`. Key changes include updating the public API to ensure Midjourney is no longer listed among available services and documents. Existing tenant sites remain unaffected, maintaining their current configurations.

items.title: Launch of Node Daemon for Remote Claude Code in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: The new node daemon for the Coding Bridge Agent allows users to run Claude Code locally while controlling it through the AceDataCloud web app or mobile devices. Key features include local execution, outbound-only connections, and a secure pairing process that ensures user permissions are respected. This implementation is designed to enhance user privacy and security by keeping all code execution and file access local to the user's machine.

items.title: Revamped coding-bridge Implementation in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update clarifies that coding-bridge and Nexior are newly developed under the MIT license, with only the Node daemon adapting GPL code from VibeBridge. Key changes include reframing the project as a reference architecture and ensuring that coding-bridge is not mischaracterized as a GPL derivative. The licensing and reuse tables have also been updated to reflect these changes.

items.title: Enhance Mongo Archive Deployment with Resilience in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a continuous TKE Deployment for the Suno Mongo archive job, ensuring persistent runner progress is saved to COS. Key additions include management helpers for one-shot and deployment operations, utilizing internal Mongo and COS endpoints in Hong Kong. The deployment is currently active with a durable checkpoint system for improved reliability.

items.title: Introducing Auto Top-up Technical Plan in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a comprehensive technical plan for the Auto Top-up feature, detailed down to the function level in the new `.plans/AUTO-RECHARGE.md` file. The system will automatically recharge user balances using authorized payment methods when they fall below a set threshold, enhancing user experience by eliminating manual payment processes. Key components include a new data model, backend endpoints, and risk control measures.

items.title: Introducing coding-bridge: A New WebSocket Relay in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces **coding-bridge**, a stateful WebSocket relay service that allows authenticated browsers to control Claude Code and Codex sessions on users' local machines. Key features include secure Ace JWT verification, a robust MongoDB persistence layer, and a device-flow pairing mechanism. This service enhances user experience by maintaining tool execution on remote nodes while ensuring ownership isolation and security.

items.title: Introducing Coding Bridge Design in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a comprehensive design document for the Coding Bridge, a system enabling remote control of Claude Code/Codex from web and mobile devices. Key features include a secure architecture leveraging Ace JWT and MongoDB, a QR device-flow for pairing, and a phased implementation plan. The project builds on the VibeBridge framework, ensuring robust permission management and multi-session capabilities.

items.title: Enable Auto Recharge by Default in Frontend Web ⚡
items.summary: The Auto Recharge feature is now displayed by default in the console sidebar, allowing direct access without feature flag restrictions. Additionally, the Auto Recharge button is visible on application quick access cards, enhancing user experience. Menu comments in both English and Chinese have been updated to reflect these changes.

items.title: Enhance OpenAI Image Edit Size Inference in Platform Service 🎨
items.summary: This update improves the inference of image edit sizes for OpenAI by deriving dimensions from the first reference image. It ensures that the inferred sizes are valid multiples of 16, adhering to gpt-image-2 limits, and forwards these sizes in edit requests. Key files have been modified and new tests added to validate the functionality.
